subsections.heading: Purpose
subsections.content: The purpose of SentimentScope is to analyze public sentiment from social media posts.

subsections.heading: Intended Audience
subsections.content:
This document is intended for:
Manager
Analyst
Development Team
Stakeholders

subsections.heading: Included in scope:
subsections.content:
Data scraping
Sentiment analysis
Visualization charts

subsections.heading: Excluded from scope:
subsections.content:
Hardware manufacturing
Third-party marketing

subsections.heading: Document Conventions
subsections.content:
Standard IEEE formatting
Bold text for UI elements

subsections.heading: References
subsections.content: GDPR: General Data Protection Regulation

subsections.heading: Product Perspective
subsections.content: SentimentScope is a standalone Web Application for the Marketing / Analytics sector, designed to analyze public sentiment from social media posts.

subsections.heading: Product Features
subsections.content:
Data scraping
Sentiment analysis
Visualization charts

subsections.heading: Manager
subsections.content:
Tech-savvy
Frequent use for strategic decisions

subsections.heading: Analyst
subsections.content:
Highly tech-savvy
Frequent use for data analysis

subsections.heading: Operating Environment
subsections.content: Hybrid deployment environment

subsections.heading: Design and Implementation Constraints
subsections.content:
Backend implementation in Go
GDPR compliance
Hybrid deployment

subsections.heading: User Documentation
subsections.content:
User Manual
API Documentation

subsections.heading: Assumptions:
subsections.content: Users have access to social media platforms

subsections.heading: Dependencies:
subsections.content:
Social media APIs for data scraping
Go backend framework

subsections.heading: User Authentication
subsections.content: Description: Authenticates users and authorizes access to the system.

subsections.heading: Stimulus/Response Sequences:
subsections.content:
Stimulus: User submits login credentials
Response: System verifies credentials and grants access.

subsections.heading: Functional Requirements:
subsections.content:
The system shall validate user credentials against a database.
The system shall generate a session token upon successful authentication.

subsections.heading: Data Scraping
subsections.content: Description: Extracts data from social media posts based on user input.

subsections.heading: Stimulus/Response Sequences:
subsections.content:
Stimulus: Analyst inputs a keyword
Response: System scrapes social media posts related to the keyword.

subsections.heading: Functional Requirements:
subsections.content:
The system shall use a web scraping library to extract data from social media platforms.
The system shall store scraped data in a database for further analysis.

subsections.heading: Sentiment Analysis
subsections.content: Description: Analyzes the sentiment of scraped social media posts.

subsections.heading: Stimulus/Response Sequences:
subsections.content:
Stimulus: System receives scraped social media posts
Response: System analyzes the sentiment of the posts.

subsections.heading: Functional Requirements:
subsections.content:
The system shall use a natural language processing library to analyze sentiment.
The system shall categorize sentiment as positive, negative, or neutral.

subsections.heading: Visualization Charts
subsections.content: Description: Displays sentiment analysis results in a graphical format.

subsections.heading: Stimulus/Response Sequences:
subsections.content:
Stimulus: System completes sentiment analysis
Response: System generates visualization charts.

subsections.heading: Functional Requirements:
subsections.content:
The system shall use a charting library to generate bar charts and pie charts.
The system shall display charts in a user-friendly dashboard.

subsections.heading: User Interfaces
subsections.content: The SentimentScope web application features a responsive design built with React 18 and TypeScript. It supports modern web browsers (Chrome 90+, Firefox 88+, Safari 14+) and mobile devices (iOS 14+, Android 10+). The interface includes a real-time dashboard with WebSocket updates, role-based access control (Analyst, Manager), and progressive web app capabilities with service worker caching.

subsections.heading: Hardware Interfaces
subsections.content: This software system operates entirely in a cloud-native environment with no direct hardware integrations. All infrastructure is virtualized and managed through containerization (Docker/Kubernetes).

subsections.heading: Software Interfaces
subsections.content: The system uses a Go-based backend with a hybrid deployment preference. It integrates with external services for authentication and data storage, but specific software interfaces are not detailed in the provided SRS.

subsections.heading: Communication Interfaces
subsections.content: All client-server communication is encrypted via TLS. The system uses REST APIs for data exchange and WebSocket for real-time updates. Specific communication protocols and security layers are not detailed in the provided SRS.

subsections.heading: Performance Requirements
subsections.content:
The system shall support 100 to 1,000 concurrent users without performance degradation.
Rationale: To accommodate expected peak traffic during marketing campaigns.
The system shall respond to user requests in real-time.
Rationale: To ensure a responsive user experience and meet industry standards.

subsections.heading: Security Requirements
subsections.content:
The system shall implement authentication for all users.
Rationale: To ensure that only authorized users can access the system.
The system shall comply with GDPR requirements.
Rationale: To protect sensitive user information and meet regulatory requirements.

subsections.heading: Quality Attributes
subsections.content:
The application must achieve high availability.
Rationale: To ensure reliability for enterprise-level clients.
The system shall be maintainable with modular architecture and comprehensive documentation.
Rationale: To enable efficient updates and reduce long-term maintenance costs.

subsections.heading: Technical Terms
subsections.content:
API: Application Programming Interface - a set of protocols and tools for building software applications.
Go: A statically typed, compiled programming language used for developing the backend of the system.
GDPR: General Data Protection Regulation - a regulation in EU law on data protection and privacy.
Hybrid Deployment: A deployment environment that combines on-premises and cloud-based infrastructure.
Web Scraping: The process of automatically extracting data from web pages.
Sentiment Analysis: A natural language processing technique used to determine the emotional tone or attitude conveyed by a piece of text.

subsections.heading: Acronyms and Abbreviations
subsections.content:
GDPR: General Data Protection Regulation
API: Application Programming Interface

subsections.heading: System Components
subsections.content:
Authentication Service: Component responsible for verifying user credentials and managing access tokens.
Database Layer: Persistent storage layer managing data operations and ensuring data integrity.

subsections.heading: Technologies
subsections.content:
Go: Programming language used for developing the backend of the system.
Cloud Service Providers: Third-party services that provide cloud-based infrastructure for deployment and scaling.

subsections.heading: Performance Metrics
subsections.content:
Concurrent Users: Number of users actively using the system simultaneously without performance degradation.
Response Time: Time elapsed between a user request and the system's response.

subsections.heading: Security Terms
subsections.content:
Authentication: Process of verifying the identity of a user or system.
Authorization: Process of determining whether a user has access to a particular resource or functionality.
Encryption: Process of converting plaintext data into unreadable ciphertext to protect it from unauthorized access.

subsections.heading: Quality Attributes
subsections.content:
Reliability: Ability of the system to perform its required functions under normal operating conditions.
Scalability: Ability of the system to handle increased load or demand without decreasing performance.
Maintainability: Ease with which the system can be maintained, updated, or modified.

subsections.heading: Assumption 1:
subsections.content:
Users have access to social media platforms.
Impact: If users do not have access to social media platforms, the system will not be able to scrape data, leading to incomplete sentiment analysis and reduced functionality.

subsections.heading: Assumption 2:
subsections.content:
The social media APIs used for data scraping will maintain a stable and compatible interface.
Impact: If the social media APIs change or become unstable, the system's data scraping functionality may break, requiring immediate updates to adapt to the changes.

subsections.heading: Assumption 3:
subsections.content:
The Go backend framework will be available and compatible with the chosen database.
Impact: If the Go backend framework or database compatibility issues arise, the system's development timeline may be delayed, and additional resources may be required to resolve the issues.

subsections.heading: Assumption 4:
subsections.content:
The system will be deployed in a hybrid environment with reliable internet connectivity.
Impact: If the hybrid environment experiences connectivity issues or downtime, the system's availability and performance may be negatively impacted.

subsections.heading: Assumption 5:
subsections.content:
Users possess basic computer literacy and familiarity with web-based applications.
Impact: If users lack basic digital skills, additional training programs and simplified UI/UX may be required, increasing project costs and extending the rollout timeline.

subsections.heading: Assumption 6:
subsections.content:
The cloud service provider will maintain the agreed-upon pricing structure for compute and storage resources.
Impact: Significant price increases could exceed budget constraints, requiring migration to alternative providers or optimization of resource usage to reduce costs.

subsections.heading: Assumption 7:
subsections.content:
Regulatory requirements for data privacy (GDPR, CCPA) will remain stable during development.
Impact: Changes in data privacy regulations could necessitate significant architectural changes, additional compliance features, and potential project delays.

subsections.heading: Assumption 8:
subsections.content:
The database server will have sufficient storage capacity with the ability to expand.
Impact: Insufficient storage capacity would limit data retention capabilities and could cause system failures if storage limits are reached unexpectedly.

raw_text:
Software Requirements Specification
for
SentimentScope
Prepared by Kunal Verma, Dhruvil Joshi
Organization: Charotar University of Science and Technology
Date Created: 02/02/2026
Table of Contents
[Note: The Table of Contents will be automatically generated when you open this document in Microsoft Word. If it doesn't appear, right-click and select 'Update Field']
1. Introduction
1.1 Purpose
The purpose of SentimentScope is to analyze public sentiment from social media posts.
1.2 Intended Audience
This document is intended for:
Manager
Analyst
Development Team
Stakeholders
1.3 Project Scope
Included in scope:
Data scraping
Sentiment analysis
Visualization charts
Excluded from scope:
Hardware manufacturing
Third-party marketing
1.4 Document Conventions
Standard IEEE formatting
Bold text for UI elements
1.5 References
GDPR: General Data Protection Regulation
2. Overall Description
2.1 Product Perspective
SentimentScope is a standalone Web Application for the Marketing / Analytics sector, designed to analyze public sentiment from social media posts.
2.2 Product Features
Data scraping
Sentiment analysis
Visualization charts
2.3 User Classes and Characteristics
Manager
Tech-savvy
Frequent use for strategic decisions
Analyst
Highly tech-savvy
Frequent use for data analysis
2.4 Operating Environment
Hybrid deployment environment
2.5 Design and Implementation Constraints
Backend implementation in Go
GDPR compliance
Hybrid deployment
2.6 User Documentation
User Manual
API Documentation
2.7 Assumptions and Dependencies
Assumptions:
Users have access to social media platforms
Dependencies:
Social media APIs for data scraping
Go backend framework
3. System Features
3.1 User Authentication
Description: Authenticates users and authorizes access to the system.
Stimulus/Response Sequences:
Stimulus: User submits login credentials
Response: System verifies credentials and grants access.
Functional Requirements:
The system shall validate user credentials against a database.
The system shall generate a session token upon successful authentication.
3.2 Data Scraping
Description: Extracts data from social media posts based on user input.
Stimulus/Response Sequences:
Stimulus: Analyst inputs a keyword
Response: System scrapes social media posts related to the keyword.
Functional Requirements:
The system shall use a web scraping library to extract data from social media platforms.
The system shall store scraped data in a database for further analysis.
3.3 Sentiment Analysis
Description: Analyzes the sentiment of scraped social media posts.
Stimulus/Response Sequences:
Stimulus: System receives scraped social media posts
Response: System analyzes the sentiment of the posts.
Functional Requirements:
The system shall use a natural language processing library to analyze sentiment.
The system shall categorize sentiment as positive, negative, or neutral.
3.4 Visualization Charts
Description: Displays sentiment analysis results in a graphical format.
Stimulus/Response Sequences:
Stimulus: System completes sentiment analysis
Response: System generates visualization charts.
Functional Requirements:
The system shall use a charting library to generate bar charts and pie charts.
The system shall display charts in a user-friendly dashboard.
4. External Interface Requirements
4.1 User Interfaces
The SentimentScope web application features a responsive design built with React 18 and TypeScript. It supports modern web browsers (Chrome 90+, Firefox 88+, Safari 14+) and mobile devices (iOS 14+, Android 10+). The interface includes a real-time dashboard with WebSocket updates, role-based access control (Analyst, Manager), and progressive web app capabilities with service worker caching.
User Interface Architecture:
4.2 Hardware Interfaces
This software system operates entirely in a cloud-native environment with no direct hardware integrations. All infrastructure is virtualized and managed through containerization (Docker/Kubernetes).
Hardware Interface Architecture:
4.3 Software Interfaces
The system uses a Go-based backend with a hybrid deployment preference. It integrates with external services for authentication and data storage, but specific software interfaces are not detailed in the provided SRS.
Software Interface Architecture:
4.4 Communication Interfaces
All client-server communication is encrypted via TLS. The system uses REST APIs for data exchange and WebSocket for real-time updates. Specific communication protocols and security layers are not detailed in the provided SRS.
Communication Interface Architecture:
5. Non-Functional Requirements
5.1 Performance Requirements
The system shall support 100 to 1,000 concurrent users without performance degradation.
Rationale: To accommodate expected peak traffic during marketing campaigns.
The system shall respond to user requests in real-time.
Rationale: To ensure a responsive user experience and meet industry standards.
5.2 Safety Requirements
5.3 Security Requirements
The system shall implement authentication for all users.
Rationale: To ensure that only authorized users can access the system.
The system shall comply with GDPR requirements.
Rationale: To protect sensitive user information and meet regulatory requirements.
5.4 Quality Attributes
The application must achieve high availability.
Rationale: To ensure reliability for enterprise-level clients.
The system shall be maintainable with modular architecture and comprehensive documentation.
Rationale: To enable efficient updates and reduce long-term maintenance costs.
6. Glossary
6.1 Technical Terms
API: Application Programming Interface - a set of protocols and tools for building software applications.
Go: A statically typed, compiled programming language used for developing the backend of the system.
GDPR: General Data Protection Regulation - a regulation in EU law on data protection and privacy.
Hybrid Deployment: A deployment environment that combines on-premises and cloud-based infrastructure.
Web Scraping: The process of automatically extracting data from web pages.
Sentiment Analysis: A natural language processing technique used to determine the emotional tone or attitude conveyed by a piece of text.
6.2 Acronyms and Abbreviations
GDPR: General Data Protection Regulation
API: Application Programming Interface
6.3 System Components
Authentication Service: Component responsible for verifying user credentials and managing access tokens.
Database Layer: Persistent storage layer managing data operations and ensuring data integrity.
6.4 Technologies
Go: Programming language used for developing the backend of the system.
Cloud Service Providers: Third-party services that provide cloud-based infrastructure for deployment and scaling.
6.5 Performance Metrics
Concurrent Users: Number of users actively using the system simultaneously without performance degradation.
Response Time: Time elapsed between a user request and the system's response.
6.6 Security Terms
Authentication: Process of verifying the identity of a user or system.
Authorization: Process of determining whether a user has access to a particular resource or functionality.
Encryption: Process of converting plaintext data into unreadable ciphertext to protect it from unauthorized access.
6.7 Quality Attributes
Reliability: Ability of the system to perform its required functions under normal operating conditions.
Scalability: Ability of the system to handle increased load or demand without decreasing performance.
Maintainability: Ease with which the system can be maintained, updated, or modified.
7. Assumptions
Assumption 1:
Users have access to social media platforms.
Impact: If users do not have access to social media platforms, the system will not be able to scrape data, leading to incomplete sentiment analysis and reduced functionality.
Assumption 2:
The social media APIs used for data scraping will maintain a stable and compatible interface.
Impact: If the social media APIs change or become unstable, the system's data scraping functionality may break, requiring immediate updates to adapt to the changes.
Assumption 3:
The Go backend framework will be available and compatible with the chosen database.
Impact: If the Go backend framework or database compatibility issues arise, the system's development timeline may be delayed, and additional resources may be required to resolve the issues.
Assumption 4:
The system will be deployed in a hybrid environment with reliable internet connectivity.
Impact: If the hybrid environment experiences connectivity issues or downtime, the system's availability and performance may be negatively impacted.
Assumption 5:
Users possess basic computer literacy and familiarity with web-based applications.
Impact: If users lack basic digital skills, additional training programs and simplified UI/UX may be required, increasing project costs and extending the rollout timeline.
Assumption 6:
The cloud service provider will maintain the agreed-upon pricing structure for compute and storage resources.
Impact: Significant price increases could exceed budget constraints, requiring migration to alternative providers or optimization of resource usage to reduce costs.
Assumption 7:
Regulatory requirements for data privacy (GDPR, CCPA) will remain stable during development.
Impact: Changes in data privacy regulations could necessitate significant architectural changes, additional compliance features, and potential project delays.
Assumption 8:
The database server will have sufficient storage capacity with the ability to expand.
Impact: Insufficient storage capacity would limit data retention capabilities and could cause system failures if storage limits are reached unexpectedly.
